task_completion.description: Verify that the Vertex classes 'Conferences' and 'Sessions' were created with their required properties.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OrientDB Studio Schema tab or console output showing 'Conferences' (with 'Name' property) and 'Sessions' (with 'Title' and 'Duration' properties) listed as Vertex classes.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Edge classes 'HostedAt', 'HasSession', and 'PresentedBy' were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OrientDB Studio Schema tab or console output showing 'HostedAt', 'HasSession', and 'PresentedBy' listed as Edge classes.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Conference data was successfully ingested into the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a query result (e.g., 'SELECT * FROM Conferences') showing exactly 2 records: 'Global Graph Summit 2026' and 'Travel Tech World'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Session data was successfully ingested into the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a query result (e.g., 'SELECT * FROM Sessions') showing exactly 4 session records (e.g., 'Scaling OrientDB Clusters', 'Graph Algorithms in Practice', etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify that 'HostedAt' edges link Conferences to Hotels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph visualization or query output showing 'HostedAt' edges originating from Conference vertices and pointing to Hotel vertices (e.g., 'Global Graph Summit 2026' -> 'Hotel Artemide').

task_completion.description: Verify that 'HasSession' edges link Conferences to Sessions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph visualization or query output showing 'HasSession' edges originating from Conference vertices and pointing to Session vertices.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'PresentedBy' edges link Sessions to Profiles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph visualization or query output showing 'PresentedBy' edges originating from Session vertices and pointing to Profile vertices (e.g., linked to 'luca.rossi@example.com').

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OrientDB database (via Studio web interface or console) rather than just editing text files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the OrientDB Studio UI, OrientDB console prompts, or executed scripts interacting with the DB, not just a text editor showing JSON or SQL commands without execution.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just manually type out a mock success message or hardcode the final output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual query execution results, record IDs (RIDs like #12:0), or graph visualizations that indicate real database records were created.

integrity.description: Ensure the ingested data matches the provided JSON file and wasn't hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The conference names, session titles, and presenter emails in the database queries/graph must match the specific synthetic data provided in the privileged info.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source data is located at '/home/ga/conference_data.json'. The schema must include 5 specific classes: Vertices 'Conferences' and 'Sessions', and Edges 'HostedAt', 'HasSession', and 'PresentedBy'. The database should contain exactly 2 Conferences ('Global Graph Summit 2026' and 'Travel Tech World') and 4 Sessions. 'Global Graph Summit 2026' is hosted at 'Hotel Artemide' and has 2 sessions ('Scaling OrientDB Clusters' [45m], 'Graph Algorithms in Practice' [60m]). 'Travel Tech World' is hosted at 'The Savoy' and has 2 sessions ('Recommender Systems 101' [30m], 'The Future of Booking' [50m]). Presenters include luca.rossi@example.com, sophie.martin@example.com, and david.jones@example.com.
